references.text:
[Br19] Bremner, Andrew, On a problem of Erd\H{o}s related to common factor
differences. Int. J. Number Theory (2019), 1059--1068.
 
 [ErRo97] Erd\H{o}s, Paul and Rosenfeld, Moshe, The factor-difference set of integers. Acta Arith. (1997), 353--359.
 
 [Ji99] Jim\'{e}nez-Urroz, Jorge, A note on a conjecture of Erd\H{o}s and {R}osenfeld. J. Number Theory (1999), 140--143.

remarks.text: A question of Erd\H{o}s and Rosenfeld \cite{ErRo97}, who proved this is true for $k=2$. Jim\'{e}nez-Urroz \cite{Ji99} proved this for $k=3$ and Bremner \cite{Br19} proved this for $k=4$.

statement.text: For integer $n\geq 1$ we define the factor difference set of $n$ by\[D(n) = \{\lvert a-b\rvert : n=ab\}.\]Is it true that, for every $k\geq 1$, there exist integers $N_1<\cdots<N_k$ such that\[\lvert \cap_i D(N_i)\rvert \geq k?\]
